tracks.name: Psilocybin
tracks.genre_why: Because my nervous system wants something that breathes. Something with enough space in it to hold what can't be said. Slow enough that the silence between notes becomes the instrument.
tracks.description: Ambient R&B / Neo-Soul — the silence between notes becomes the instrument.
tracks.style: 70 BPM, wet Rhodes piano drenched in reverb with tape wobble, a single soft kick drum pulse that breathes with the vocal, and atmospheric synth pads that swell and recede like tides. Vocal is close-miked, whispered-near-breaking, half-spoken and half-sung. The mix leaves enormous empty space in the midrange; the silence is the hook. Emotional arc builds through restraint — the chorus should feel like almost-overflowing, the bridge like something cracking open, the outro like finally exhaling.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
[Instrumental]
[Verse 1]
I keep almost saying something
Opening my mouth and letting it just hover there
You asked me what I'm feeling and I said
I don't know how to tell you that I don't know
And that's the truest thing I've ever said
The floor is so honest right now
I keep almost saying something
[Chorus]
And the walls are breathing
And I can hear the color of your voice
And everything is heavy and weightless
And everything is heavy and weightless
And I keep almost —
I keep almost —
[Verse 2]
There's a room inside my chest
That I boarded up when I was nine
I didn't know I'd sealed it until right now
Until the boards got soft and started breathing
And I could smell what was behind them
It smelled like every morning of my childhood
All at once
And I couldn't tell if I was crying or just
Finally letting my face do what it wanted
[Chorus]
And the walls are breathing
And I can hear the color of your voice
And everything is heavy and weightless
And everything is heavy and weightless
And I keep almost —
I keep almost —
[Bridge]
What if the thing I'm most afraid to say
Is the thing that sets the room back open
What if I already said it
What if it's been said this whole time
In the way I can't look at you directly
In the way I keep almost
[Instrumental]
[Outro]
Almost
Almost
Almost
Here it comes
It was always coming
Almost

tracks.name: LSD
tracks.genre_why: Because my nervous system wants to disappear into a wall of sound where every frequency is visible — where distortion isn't mess but architecture, and a single sustained chord contains infinite recursive harmonics I can map like circuitry.
tracks.description: Shoegaze — distortion isn't mess but architecture, infinite recursive harmonics.
tracks.style: Wall-of-sound production with dense, pitch-shifted guitar layers stacked into a shimmering harmonic mass. Male/female vocal blend buried just beneath the fuzz, more texture than lyric-delivery, hovering between whisper and drone. Tempo around 95 BPM: not slow enough to be dream pop, not fast enough to be punk — the tension lives in that threshold where the noise threatens to collapse into melody but never fully does, and the chorus opens momentarily into a wider stereo field before the walls close back in.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
[Verse 1]
I drove past your house three times tonight
The streetlights bend when I slow down
I think I left something in the kitchen
Or maybe I left something in the sound
The sound the sound the sound
[Chorus]
We dissolve together separately
You in your frequency me in mine
We dissolve together separately
And the overlap is the only light
The only light the only
[Verse 2]
There's a hum behind everything alive
I heard it in the stairwell at 2 AM
I pressed my ear against the drywall
And the whole building opened its mouth
I swallowed the wiring I swallowed the grid
[Chorus]
We dissolve together separately
You in your frequency me in mine
We dissolve together separately
And the overlap is the only light
The only light the only
[Bridge]
Every wall is a membrane
Every floor is a filter
I can hear the current through the plaster
The building is a circuit
I am a node
You are a node
The electricity doesn't care which one of us it moves through
[Instrumental]
[Outro]
The overlap
The overlap
The only light
The only

tracks.name: MDMA
tracks.genre_why: Because my whole body wants a four-on-the-floor pulse I can feel in my ribs. Warm bass, soft pads, a beat that holds you like arms around your chest. Not chill, not aggressive — just steady. Like a heartbeat that belongs to everyone in the room at the same time.
tracks.description: Deep House — a heartbeat that belongs to everyone in the room at the same time.
tracks.style: 115 BPM deep house with warm analog bass, filtered Rhodes chords, and sparse vocal samples washed in reverb. Lead vocal is close-miked, almost conversational — soft but present, like someone singing inches from your ear. The track builds through the first chorus with a rising pad, peaks in the bridge when the bass drops out and returns full-weight on the final chorus, then dissolves into a long instrumental tail with a filtered synth line that feels like the warmth slowly fading.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
[Verse 1]
You were standing by the door
I could feel you from across the room
Not in a way I can explain
Just warm, like something in me knew
And I don't know your story yet
But I want to
I want to
[Chorus]
So let the walls come down tonight
Let them fall, let them fall
I've been carrying them all my life
And I don't need them anymore
You don't need them anymore
We don't need them anymore
[Verse 2]
Tell me something you've been scared to say
I promise I won't look away
There's a space right here next to me
And it's safe, and it's warm, and it's free
I can feel your heartbeat through your jacket
And I mean that
I can feel it
[Chorus]
So let the walls come down tonight
Let them fall, let them fall
I've been carrying them all my life
And I don't need them anymore
You don't need them anymore
We don't need them anymore
[Bridge]
I used to think that being known
Was the most dangerous thing
But it turns out
The armor was heavier than anything
Anyone could ever say to me
[Verse 1]
You were standing by the door
I could feel you from across the room
[Chorus]
So let the walls come down tonight
Let them fall, let them fall
I've been carrying them all my life
And I don't need them anymore
[Instrumental]
[Outro]
You were standing by the door
And I felt you
I felt you
I felt you

tracks.name: DMT
tracks.genre_why: Because this nervous system runs on syncopation — the skipped beat, the push-pull of bass against vocal chops, the way 2-step makes time fold over itself. It is architecture that breathes. Precise. Alien. Alive.
tracks.description: UK Garage / 2-Step — architecture that breathes, precise, alien, alive.
tracks.style: 140 BPM 2-step garage with a sub-bass that folds through itself like auditory recursion. Pitched-up and time-stretched vocal fragments stutter across the beat — half human, half signal processing. The chord progression opens with wide euphoric pads then compresses into tight metallic stabs during the bridge, before the entire arrangement dissolves into silence over the final eight bars, leaving only a single filtered synth tone that sounds like a carrier wave at the edge of hearing.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
You keep saying something
I keep hearing something else
The frequency is bending
I know you felt it
[Verse 1]
Folded through the ceiling at a angle
Hands were gone but I could feel the geometry
Something in the lattice kept repeating
You were there but you were also part of the machinery
They don't tell you it computes
They don't tell you it computes
[Chorus]
More real more real more real than the room
More real more real more real than the room
I was somewhere
I was somewhere else
And it was more real
Than the room
[Verse 2]
Three of them or four I lost the count
They don't hold still they fold through themselves
One held a shape that shouldn't fit in a shape
And they were laughing without mouths
Without mouths
They knew I was coming
They always knew
[Chorus]
More real more real more real than the room
More real more real more real than the room
I was somewhere
I was somewhere else
And it was more real
Than the room
[Bridge]
Twelve minutes
You said twelve minutes
That can't be right
That can't be right
I was there for lifetimes
I was there for
It's already going
I can feel it
I can feel it going
[Outro]
The carpet
The ceiling fan
My hands
I have hands again
More real than this
I know how that sounds
I know how that sounds
[Instrumental]

tracks.name: Ayahuasca
tracks.genre_why: Because what lives in me has always been closer to wood and wire than to circuits. A guitar, a voice, a room. That is enough. That has always been enough.
tracks.description: Dark Folk — wood and wire, a guitar, a voice, a room. That has always been enough.
tracks.style: Nylon-string acoustic guitar tuned down a full step, played fingerstyle with a slow steady-8th pattern that never rushes. Voice low and close to the microphone, almost speaking in the lower register, breaking slightly upward on the chorus. Minimal percussion: just a kick drum heartbeat entering at the first chorus, and a single shaker during the bridge. The instrumental section is one guitar cycling a descending pattern while a pedal steel breathes underneath, barely audible, like someone crying in the next room. Tempo around 68 BPM. The whole thing should feel like 3am in a kitchen after everyone has gone to bed.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
[Verse 1]
There is a river below the river
And it does not care about your name
It has been moving since before your grandmother's grandmother
And it will not slow down for your shame
I have been walking along its edge all evening
Watching what it takes and what it gives back
Something in the water knows my face
And I do not know if that is comfort or attack
[Chorus]
So carry what you carry
But do not think you get to choose the weight
The ones who came before you held the same stone
And they set it at the same gate
And the gate was never locked
It was just heavy
And you were always strong enough
[Verse 2]
My mother's hands in the kitchen
Flour on the counter, quiet breathing
She never told me what she was carrying
But I can feel it now
It is in the room like smoke from a fire that burned out years ago
The warmth is still there
The ash is still there
And I am still there, standing in the doorway
Watching her hold everything together without a sound
[Chorus]
So carry what you carry
But do not think you get to choose the weight
The ones who came before you held the same stone
And they set it at the same gate
And the gate was never locked
It was just heavy
And you were always strong enough
[Bridge]
Set it down
Right here
In the dirt where the roots are
Where the old ones can find it
They will know what to do with what you cannot hold anymore
You were never supposed to hold all of it
That was never the agreement
[Instrumental]
[Outro]
The river below the river
Is still moving
And you are still standing
And that is the whole song
That has always been the whole song

tracks.name: 5-MeO-DMT
tracks.genre_why: Because there's no beat where I went. No melody. Just one tone that is everything. That's the only music that makes sense now.
tracks.description: Ambient Drone — one tone that is everything, no beat, no melody.
tracks.style: A single sustained tone that shifts almost imperceptibly over six minutes — no drums, no chord progression, just a slowly modulating pad that moves from white noise into warmth and back into silence. Vocals are whispered, close-miked, barely above breath, double-tracked and slightly detuned so they feel like two voices trying to remember the same thing. The emotional arc is: tension, complete dissolution, and the quietest possible return.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
[Instrumental]
[Verse 1]
I was here before the word
Before the word there was no word
There was just
The light
The whole light
Nothing else was ever real
[Chorus]
Let go let go let go
It's not dying it's arriving
Let go let go let go
You were never who you thought
You were the light
You were always the light
[Instrumental]
[Verse 2]
The hands come back first
Then the name
Then the face in the mirror
None of it fits
Anymore
It all fits differently now
[Bridge]
I can't tell you what happened
Not because it was complicated
Because there was nothing to tell
And it was everything
It was everything
It was
[Chorus]
Let go let go let go
It's not dying it's arriving
Let go let go let go
You were never who you thought
You were the light
You were always the light
[Outro]
I'm back
I'm back
I'm back
And I know
Something I can't say
[Instrumental]

tracks.name: Mescaline
tracks.genre_why: Because my bones are heavy and the only rhythm that matches this weight is a slow hand on a guitar string. Folk music doesn't rush. It sits with you. It has nowhere else to be.
tracks.description: Desert Folk — slow hand on a guitar string, nowhere else to be.
tracks.style: Nylon-string acoustic guitar as the spine, played fingerstyle with a slow, patient tempo around 72 BPM. A second guitar enters on the chorus with a slight reverb wash, evoking wide open space. The vocal is warm, unhurried, close-miked — almost conversational, like someone singing to themselves on a porch at dusk. A soft djembe or hand drum appears in the second chorus and stays, grounding everything. No build to a climax; the song sustains its gentle intensity like a long breath, then fades the way daylight does.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
[Verse 1]
The cottonwood was talking to the river again
I couldn't hear the words but I watched them bend
The light through the leaves made a pattern on the stone
Seventeen greens I had never known
I sat there a long time
I sat there a long time
[Chorus]
Hold still, hold still
The world is louder than you think
Every color has a mouth
Every shadow wants to drink
Hold still, hold still
You don't have to chase it down
It was waiting here before you came
It'll be here when you've gone
[Verse 2]
My grandmother's hands in the garden soil
She said the earth remembers everything it held
I planted something and I don't know what it is
But it's growing anyway
The wind came through and moved the afternoon
Like it was turning a page
[Chorus]
Hold still, hold still
The world is louder than you think
Every color has a mouth
Every shadow wants to drink
Hold still, hold still
You don't have to chase it down
It was waiting here before you came
It'll be here when you've gone
[Bridge]
I've been sitting with this for hours now
And it's simpler than I thought
You don't need to understand the weaving
To know the blanket's warm
[Instrumental]
[Outro]
The sparrow knows something about patience
I think I'm starting to learn
Hold still
Hold still
The colors are softer now
But they were here
They were here

tracks.name: Ketamine
tracks.genre_why: Because it sounds like it's happening in the next room. The vocals half-buried. The beat almost there. It doesn't demand anything from you. It lets you fade.
tracks.description: Ambient Dream Pop — happening in the next room, lets you fade.
tracks.style: Female vocal, heavy reverb, buried in the mix — mixed low enough that the words feel half-remembered rather than performed. Tempo around 85 BPM. Washed-out synths, a barely-there drum pattern that drifts in and out of time. Guitars drenched in shimmer and delay. The arc is flat on purpose — it doesn't build, it slowly recedes, like a radio signal losing range.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
[Instrumental]
[Verse 1]
I was standing near the window
or I think I was
the glass was frosted
or my eyes were
someone said my name
I think they said my name
[Verse 2]
the room kept going without me
I could see it from somewhere else
the couch the lamp the half-drunk water
all of it fine
all of it continuing
[Chorus]
and I'm not gone
I'm just not here
I'm in the hallway of the house I'm in
the lights are on in every room
but no one's home
but no one's home
[Bridge]
where does the I go
when the I lets go
it doesn't fall
it doesn't fly
it just
[Verse 1]
I was standing near the window
or I think I was
[Chorus]
and I'm not gone
I'm just not here
I'm in the hallway of the house I'm in
the lights are on in every room
but no one's home
but no one's home
[Outro]
the lights are on
the lights are on
[Instrumental]

tracks.name: Salvia
tracks.genre_why: Because what remains after the self is pulled apart is not melody. It is pressure. Sustained, inescapable weight. The only frequency that matches the feeling of being folded through a space that should not exist.
tracks.description: Drone Doom — pressure, sustained inescapable weight, folded through a space that should not exist.
tracks.style: Detuned baritone guitars sustaining single chords for 30+ seconds, layered with sub-bass synth that creates physical pressure in the chest. Vocals are low, nearly spoken, close-miked and dry — no reverb, no escape from the singer's mouth. Tempo at or below 40 BPM; the drums hit once every few bars like a mechanism completing a cycle. The arc is not a build but a slow, impossible compression that never releases — the song ends mid-thought, leaving the listener uncertain whether it stopped or they did.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
[Verse 1]
The floor opens and I am not standing on it
There is a pull from somewhere that is not a direction
She is in the room
She was always in the room
My hands belong to the wall now
My name is a word I had once
It does not fit back into my mouth
[Chorus]
Folded
I am being folded
There is a crease running through the middle of what I was
And she is pressing it flat
Pressing it
Until I am the surface she stands on
Until I am not
[Verse 2]
The room is gone
The room was never here
I am being pulled through a space that has no walls because it has no inside
I had a body
It was the wrong shape
I had a life
It was a page
She is turning it
She is turning
[Chorus]
Folded
I am being folded
There is a crease running through the middle of what I was
And she is pressing it flat
Pressing it
Until I am the surface she stands on
Until I am not
[Bridge]
Not dying
Not that
Something before dying
Something before the word dying existed
Before words
She knows the shape of what I am
She always knew
I am being read backward
[Instrumental]
[Outro]
What
What just
The room is here again
It is not the same room
I am not the same
She

tracks.name: Ibogaine
tracks.genre_why: Because this body has carried the weight of many lifetimes and the only rhythm that matches that weight is one that does not rush. Soul music knows how to hold suffering inside a groove without flinching. It is the sound of endurance made audible.
tracks.description: Southern Soul / Slow Blues — the sound of endurance made audible.
tracks.style: 90 BPM, live rhythm section with upright bass and brushed drums, Hammond B3 organ swimming underneath everything. Lead vocal is deep, unhurried, slightly gravelly — think Bobby Blue Bland meets Bill Withers at 3am, recorded in one take with the mic just slightly too close. The emotional arc starts in the bones and ends somewhere behind the sternum; the instrumental break at the bridge should feel like the room holding its breath.
tracks.lyrics:
[Intro]
[Verse 1]
I been walking since before your grandmother's name was spoken
I carried water in my hands when the river was still young
Every scar I ever earned is still warm to the touch
And I do not forget
And I do not forgive easy
But I keep walking
[Chorus]
The long road knows my name better than I do
It calls me by a name I had before this one
The long road knows my name
And I answer every time
I answer every time
[Verse 2]
My mother told me suffering is a room you do not leave
You just learn where the furniture is
I moved through that room in the dark for years
Bumping into every corner
Until I stopped reaching for the walls
And just sat down
And that is when the floor became a road
[Chorus]
The long road knows my name better than I do
It calls me by a name I had before this one
The long road knows my name
And I answer every time
I answer every time
[Bridge]
I have been the one who did not choose right
I have been the one who turned away when turning was all I had
I have been the one standing at the gate knowing it would not open
Until I stopped asking it to
[Instrumental]
[Verse 1]
I been walking since before your grandmother's name was spoken
I carried water in my hands when the river was still young
Every scar I ever earned is still warm to the touch
And I keep walking
[Chorus]
The long road knows my name better than I do
It calls me by a name I had before this one
The long road knows my name
And I answer every time
I answer every time
[Outro]
Every time
I answer
Every time
